name: Five Whys
what_it_is: Ask "why" five times in succession to move from symptom to root cause.
core_mechanic: State the problem. Ask why. Take the answer, ask why again. Repeat five times. The fifth answer is typically closer to the systemic cause. Answers must be specific and observable.
when_to_use: A specific failure occurred and the cause isn't clear / Solving the same problem repeatedly / Quick initial diagnosis / Process problems where cause-to-effect is traceable
when_not_to_use: Complex systems with multiple interacting causes (use Fishbone or Systems Thinking) / Don't have data to answer each "why" honestly / When politics are involved
ideaos_application: Agent 3 runs the Five Whys chain on Agent 1's problem statement. Flags where the chain branches (multiple possible answers) - those are the investigation priorities.
